segments.text: Transposable Elements

Mobile Genetic Elements: Major Concepts
Often, the phenotype is high mutation rate.  This is because these genes move
Are DNA segments that are often flanked by repeats, and may encode many types of genes
Can bring multiple drug resistance qualities to bacteria, all at once
Make up a major part of our genomes

Marcus Rhoades:
Dt is a Mutator Gene
1930’s
Marcus Rhoades discovered mutator gene in Black Mexican sweet corn

Biological causes of somatic mutation:
Transposable elements

McClintock: Ds element
1940’s
Barbara McClintock  found controlling elements.  Ds (dissociation) causes chromosome breakage at the location at which it appears.  Nobel in 1983.
McClintock: Ds element

Transposable Elements (TE)
“jumping genes”
Controlling elements
Insertion sequences
Transposons
Retroposons

Insertion Sequences
IS’s only contain genes required for mobilization of the element and insertion

E. coli Insertion Sequence (IS)
IR= Inverted Repeat
The significance is that the transposase recognizes and binds to the inverted repeat for movement

Transposons
A mobile genetic segment that contains several genes, including those for the insertion of the DNA segment into the chromosome and for mobilization of the element to other locations.

Replicative Transposition              Cut and Paste Transposition
Transposition Mechanisms
Transposons transmit antibiotic resistance in bacteria
Composite transposon
Mobile genetic elements can confer multiple drug resistance traits to bacteria simultaneously

Retrotransposons
Encode a reverse transcriptase
Transpose via reverse transcription of an RNA intermediate
Retrotransposon Movement

Human Retrotransposons
LINEs-Long interspersed sequences
SINEs-Short interspersed sequences
Total: 45%

Transposons can be very useful in genetic engineering:
Introducing genes into organisms
Insertional mutagenesis
Recombination mutagenesis

Recombination between transposon inserts can be used to generate deletion mutants

Mu
Some viruses act as transposons
Mu stands for Mutator
Mu is a temperate bacteriophage that infects E. coli
In the phage particle, it’s a 37 kb linear piece of DNA

Mu lysogenized
Mu causing a deletion mutation
Mu causing an inversion mutation

Two kinds of retrotransposons
Non-retroviral like
Retroviral like

Hybrid
Dysgenesis
Transposable Elements may accelerate speciation
Transposase needs a splicing factor that is only present in the egg.  If females are P+, they also have a transcriptional repressor that prevents transcription of P element. Females that are P- do not.  Therefore a P+ male provides transposon in and egg that is P-,  it will jump around chromosomes and cause many mutations in the developing animal.
